messages.content: Write a haiku about feeling like you are wasting your entire life working on things that you have no time left to enjoy because someone else is always there to use the things you spend your time on but never offers to help maintain it or replenish it so you have no time left to enjoy it once you spend so much time trying to build it up while someone is working in the opposite direction

Wasting days on end,
Others enjoy what's built up,
No time left to breathe.
Again

Drained, toiling away,
Neglecting joys meant to stay,
Life slips, lost in fray.
Again

Hours yield nothing,
Life's thread slipping through my hands,
Unseen hand takes all.
Again

In shadows I work,
Neglecting my own desires,
Life's dance passes by.
again

Building, but it fades,
Their presence wears away mine,
Time lost, dreams unmet.
Again

Endless toil, no rest,
They use what I create, yet
Leave me with nothing.

Write a beautiful poem about how it feels to constantly work on maintaining order and producing your ideas only to have someone else use it, steal it, and destroy it without ever refilling what they remove. No time is ever left to enjoy the fruits of your labor after constantly rebuilding the parts that are consumed by someone else. This someone doesn’t appreciate the amount that you do that they get to constantly take and benefit from

In a world, I strive to weave,
Threads of creativity, I believe,
A tapestry of order, beauty untold,
Through tireless efforts, my stories unfold.

I pour my heart into each creation,
Nurturing ideas, with love and dedication,
Only to see them claimed by another,
A thief of inspiration, hearts smothered.

They take what I've built, without remorse,
Leaving me empty, with no time to endorse,
The fruits of my labor, the joys untasted,
A cycle of depletion, a soul left wasted.

For I am the gardener of dreams unbound,
Yet this interloper, my pleasures surround,
They bask in the glory of my hard-earned fruits,
But never refill, like thieving pursuits.

Oh, the weight of frustration I bear,
As I rebuild again, with endless care,
But the time to enjoy eludes my grasp,
As this interloper continues to clasp.

Yet still, within me, resilience resides,
I'll keep creating, despite the tides,
For in the shadows, my passion thrives,
And my spirit, unyielding, ever strives.

Though they may steal, destroy my art,
I'll rebuild, with love, from my very heart,
For my true reward lies in the soulful spark,
Of my creations, shining bright, leaving a mark.
Again

In a realm of toil,
I nurture dreams with fervent care,
Crafting a world of wonder and joy.
Yet, the thief of time lurks near,
Stealing what I’ve lovingly built,
Leaving only remnants of shattered dreams.

Their hands, quick and cunning,
Seize the essence of my creation,
Claims it as their own, without remorse.
They revel in the beauty I've birthed,
But fail to refill the void they've created,
Leaving me stranded, exhausted, and forlorn.

Each moment spent rebuilding,
An offering to fickle fate,
While wistful anticipation lingers,
For a taste of the fruits that remain,
Elusive, slipping through my weary grasp.

Yet still, I persist,
For the passion within me burns,
Fueled by an unyielding spirit.
With each setback, I rise anew,
Determined to carry on, undeterred,
For the power of creation is mine to wield.

Though they may steal, destroy, and ignore,
I shall rise, stronger, evermore,
And bask in the radiance of my own craft,
For the journey of creation is its own reward,
And no thief can rob me of the joy I find,
In breathing life into the worlds I design.
Again

Once more in the depths,
I labor to build, create,
Only to see it consumed,
Stolen, destroyed, without a care.

No time to savor,
The fruit of my endeavors,
As another takes, relentlessly,
Leaving nothing to replenish.

An echo of gratitude unheard,
A plea for balance, left unanswered,
I forge ahead, against the odds,
Leaning on resilience, undeterred.

For the worth of my work transcends,
The grasp of those who plunder,
I find solace in the process itself,
In the act of bringing forth wonders.

Through the trials, I find strength,
To persevere, to rise again,
With hope, my spirit defies,
The pain of seeing my efforts wane.

In the twilight hours, I remain,
A guardian of my creations,
Knowing deep within my heart,
The value of my dedication.

So I press on, undaunted,
Creating worlds that shall endure,
For in the face of adversity,
My spirit shall always endure.
Again but make the mood more sad and defeated

In an endless cycle,
I labor, my spirit weary,
Pouring my soul into creation,
Only for it to be torn asunder.

Gone is the spark of joy,
Now replaced with sorrow's shroud,
As another claims what's mine,
Leaving emptiness in their wake.

No time left to bask,
In the fruits of my labor,
For they are devoured,
Without a thought of restitution.

A silent plea, unnoticed,
For some appreciation, understanding,
But it falls upon deaf ears,
Lost amidst the chaos of their taking.

Defeated, I rebuild,
With a heavy heart, burdened,
Knowing that it will be stolen once more,
Leaving me stripped of dreams.

In this realm of stolen moments,
I find solace, fleeting whispers,
Whispering that my efforts matter,
Even if they are lost to the wind.

So I continue, though defeated,
For my creative spirit perseveres,
Even in the face of perpetual loss,
For it is the essence of my existence.

Yet deep within, a melancholy lingers,
A sadness etched upon my soul,
As I mourn the stolen dreams,
Left with remnants of what could've been.

Write a haiku about someone who always spends their time creating things that they have worked years to achieve yet never have any time to enjoy them before someone else takes and uses it and leaves them with nothing but more work to replenish what was stolen

Creation consumed,
Fleeting moments slip away,
Emptiness remains.
